title: docker-compose.yml bridge always create  "isolate": "true"
body:
Hi I am running RHEL 9.6 stable podman 5.4.0.
My Simple docker-compose.yml always creates a isolate true network which is not what i want. 
Aslo my containers run as root (not safe I know) .


``` 
services:
  ab:
    container_name: web
    image: jwilder/whoami
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
     - 9096:8000
    networks:
      - mynetwork

networks:
  mynetwork:
    driver: bridge
    driver_opts:
      isolate: "false"

```

This the the podman inspect network.

``` 
[
     {
          "name": "ab_mynetwork",
          "id": "d1b9aef1dab85d4317482a7b9d069e2db3d76d5a0c6c4c74bdde89601d34dcc2",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman3",
          "created": "2025-10-23T00:12:50.689834111+02:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.2.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.2.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "labels": {
               "com.docker.compose.config-hash": "e5945a8baa1390f7cdb6dac4741c32104dcf40f0ef770efee170e73b27e68242",
               "com.docker.compose.network": "mynetwork",
               "com.docker.compose.project": "ab",
               "com.docker.compose.version": "2.40.2"
          },
          "options": {
               "isolate": "true"
          },
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          },
          "containers": {
               "382f814f68847446a8a088df94781a60b67c0d1b6e73be6dc0fe765919335ba4": {
                    "name": "ab",
                    "interfaces": {
                         "eth0": {
                              "subnets": [
                                   {
                                        "ipnet": "10.89.2.2/24",
                                        "gateway": "10.89.2.1"
                                   }
                              ],
                              "mac_address": "6a:1b:73:b4:86:72"
                         }
                    }
               }
          }
     }
]
```

I know I can create external network and add to docker-compose.yml as external network but would be nice to disable this from docker-compose.yml  via driver opts  "isolate": "false"

I tried aslo adding /etc/containers.conf 
```
 [network]
isolate = "false"
default_network_options = ["isolate=false"]
``` 
no luck . When using docker-compose.yml always isolates network.  

So I found also this 
pkg/api/handlers/compat/networks.go

``` 
205
206     network.Options = make(map[string]string)
207
208     // dockers bridge networks are always isolated from each other
209     if network.Driver == nettypes.BridgeNetworkDriver {
210         network.Options[nettypes.IsolateOption] = "true"
211     }
212
213     for opt, optVal := range networkCreate.Options {
214         switch opt {
215         case nettypes.MTUOption:
216             fallthrough
217         case "com.docker.network.driver.mtu":
218             network.Options[nettypes.MTUOption] = optVal
```

Is this hardcoded behaviour ? 
Thank you

comments.body:
@Luap99  Thank you so much. 
Does it  make sense to create a PR change to keep default  network.Options[nettypes.IsolateOption] = "true"  but Have something like option to respect in docker-compose.yml is posible.
Example if have  

``` networks:
  mynetwork:
    driver: bridge
    driver_opts:
      isolate: "false"
```

this will change  line 210  network.Options[nettypes.IsolateOption] = "false"
So this option is not hardcoded .

Or some other suggestion are welcome ?

Like I mentioned this would also have to take care of the inspect path which deletes the isolate option unconditionally so it is not that trivial.

Overall my plan was to make network isolation the default with podman 6 anyway and then we could just drop the docker API special case handling completely and what you want to achieve should then just work.
@Luap99  All good. Then let's close this as moved to podman 6 since we will drop docker api . Thank you so much.  For answers.
Let's keep it open until it is actually done, I tagged it 6.0 so we don't forget to actually do that.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Reopening as we still need to remove the always add/remove logic from the podman compat API.
